root_text.an4.167:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahāmoggallānena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.167:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.167:2.8: Imāsaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ paṭipadānaṁ katamaṁ te paṭipadaṁ āgamma anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuttan”ti?
root_text.an4.167:3.3: Dukkhā paṭipadā dandhābhiññā, dukkhā paṭipadā khippābhiññā, sukhā paṭipadā dandhābhiññā, sukhā paṭipadā khippābhiññā.
root_text.an4.167:3.5: Imāsaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ paṭipadānaṁ yāyaṁ paṭipadā dukkhā khippābhiññā, imaṁ me paṭipadaṁ āgamma anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuttan”ti.

translation_text.an4.167:1.1: Then Venerable Sāriputta went up to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.167:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, Sāriputta sat down to one side and said to Mahāmoggallāna:
translation_text.an4.167:2.1: “Reverend Moggallāna, there are four ways of practice.
translation_text.an4.167:2.5: pleasant practice with slow insight, and
translation_text.an4.167:2.7: These are the four ways of practice.
translation_text.an4.167:2.8: Which one of these four ways of practice did you rely on to free your mind from defilements by not grasping?”
translation_text.an4.167:3.5: I relied on the painful practice with swift insight to free my mind from defilements by not grasping.”

variant_text.an4.167:2.8: catunnaṁ paṭipadānaṁ → catassannaṁ paṭipadānaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
